title: Red Qurang

{{Quote|We will have to bear its first assault. After that we can outrun. Its mass will prevent it changing its vector quickly enough to catch us before we burrow into darkspace.|A [[Unidentified subaltern|subaltern]] of the ''Red Qurang'', on the ''Widowmaker''|<ref name="Final" />}}
The '''''Red Qurang''''' was a [[yorik coral]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] used by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Species/Legends|species]] during their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Commanded by [[Ushk Choka]], the ''Red Qurang'' traveled to the living [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] to extract former [[Executor (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|Executor]] [[Nom Anor]] in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]]. The transport was successful in its mission, ferrying Anor to [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] above the planet, though the ''Red Qurang'' was [[Space warfare/Legends|heavily damaged]] by the [[Imperial Remnant|Imperial]] [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] [[Widowmaker (frigate)|''Widowmaker'']] before Zonama Sekot jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] due to [[Sabotage of Zonama Sekot|sabotage]] inflicted upon it, taking the ''Widowmaker'' with it and sparing the ''Red Qurang'' further harm.

==Description==
Constructed with a [[Hull/Legends|hull]] made from [[yorik coral]], the ''Red Qurang'' was a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] that had the ability to jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and was able to enter and exit the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Armed with at least one [[Plasma cannon/Legends|plasma cannon]], the ''Red Qurang'' was spacious enough to transport thirty [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Warrior caste|warriors]], as well as multiple [[subaltern]]s and an [[Officer/Legends|officer]]. Smaller than a [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] in size, the [[Life support/Legends|life support]]-equipped transport vessel featured a retractable [[Entry ramp/Legends|boarding ramp]], as well as a [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] outfitted with [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]] analogs.<ref name="Final" />

==History==
{{Quote|We can stand no more of this. Another strike, and—|The subaltern, just before the ''Red Qurang'' was saved by Zonama Sekot's jump to lightspeed|<ref name="Final" />}}
[[File:NomAnor-TERC.png|thumb|right|300px|Nom Anor, the Yuuzhan Vong who had to be extracted by the ''Red Qurang'']]
Commanded by [[Ushk Choka]], the ''Red Qurang'' served as a transport during the [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] Yuuzhan Vong [[Species/Legends|species]]' [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Final" /> In [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Final Prophecy]]'' to [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]].</ref> disgraced [[Executor (Yuuzhan Vong rank)|Executor]] [[Nom Anor]] convinced Yuuzhan Vong [[Supreme Overlord]] [[Shimrra Jamaane]] that he had found the living, hyperspace–capable world of [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] and could [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] it to end the threat it posed to the [[Yuuzhan Vong empire]]'s war effort and the species' religion. In response, the ''Red Qurang'' was dispatched that [[Standard year/Legends|year]] to the [[Klasse Ephemora system/Legends|Klasse Ephemora system]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], carrying a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of warriors, to extract Anor from the living world after the agent had sabotaged it. The transport vessel arrived in the [[Star system/Legends|star system]] and entered [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] over Zonama Sekot, where it went undetected by both the planet and the [[Imperial Remnant|Imperial]] frigate [[Widowmaker (frigate)|''Widowmaker'']], which was in nearby orbit.<ref name="Final" />

Aboard the ''Red Qurang'', Choka was contacted by Anor, who persuaded the commander to extract him in seven [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]]' [[Time/Legends|time]], when the living world would be occupied with the damage caused by Anor's [[Sabotage of Zonama Sekot|sabotage]]. At the appointed time, the ''Red Qurang'' descended into the atmosphere to pick up Anor and landed near the agent's location before disgorging its complement of thirty warriors to hold off the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] who were pursuing him. The arrival of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] ''[[Jade Shadow]]'' shortly afterward forced Choka to pull the ''Red Qurang'' out as soon as Anor boarded, as the armed shuttle began to open fire on both the transport and the warriors, and the ''Red Qurang'' left the ground [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] behind to be [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the Jedi there. The Yuuzhan Vong transport was momentarily pursued by the ''Jade Shadow'' as the ''Red Qurang'' raced for orbit, but the other vessel broke off its attack.<ref name="Final" />

However, with no other Yuuzhan Vong ships to occupy the Imperial frigate, the ''Widowmaker'' [[Space warfare/Legends|intercepted]] the ''Red Qurang'' after it escaped Zonama Sekot's atmosphere, and the larger ship inflicted heavy damage on the transport as the ''Red Qurang'' tried to race past it to escape to hyperspace. The Yuuzhan Vong transport would have been destroyed had Zonama Sekot's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] not activated then due to Anor's sabotage. The living world blindly jumped away, pulling the ''Widowmaker'' along with it and thus saving the ''Red Qurang'' from the fire of the Imperial [[Warship/Legends|warship]], allowing Choka to transport Anor back to Yuuzhan Vong territory.<ref name="Final" />

==Commanders and crew==
{{Quote|Keep our present course. Secure for bombardment.|Ushk Choka|<ref name="Final" />}}
The ''Red Qurang'' was commanded by Ushk Choka, a warrior from [[Domain Choka]], and multiple subalterns [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] the vessel when it extracted Nom Anor from Zonama Sekot.<ref name="Final" />

==Behind the scenes==
The transport vessel's only appearance was in the penultimate novel of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series, [[The New Jedi Order: The Final Prophecy|''The Final Prophecy'']], written by [[Greg Keyes]] and released in [[2003]].<ref name="Final" />

==Appearances==
*''[[The New Jedi Order: The Final Prophecy]]'' {{1st}}
